target.title: fix(imessage): repair DM sender fallback before pairing
target.body:
## Summary
- repair direct-message sender when the watch payload temporarily reports the local `destination_caller_id` as `sender`
- use direct-chat participant / `chat_identifier` data already present on the payload before falling back to history recovery
- keep the repair scoped to non-group, non-from-me direct messages where `sender` equals `destination_caller_id`

## Why
`imsg watch` can surface a just-inserted DM row before `handle_id` has resolved. In that window, the payload can fall back to the local send identity as `sender`. OpenClaw then treats the local account as the inbound sender and emits a bogus pairing challenge for the bot owner/local identity instead of the actual peer.

The watch payload already carries enough direct-chat context to recover the peer sender in this narrow race, so this restores `sender` before access-control/pairing logic sees the message.

I did not find an existing OpenClaw issue matching `destination_caller_id`, sender fallback, or bogus iMessage pairing identity.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts` (8 passed)

target.title: fix(whatsapp): suppress typing for unmentioned group runs regardless of delivery mode
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**

On silent agent runs, `signalTextDelta` fires `sendPresenceUpdate("composing")` if the model streams renderable text before the silent-token decision. Once fired, composing is never retracted. Group members see a persistent "typing..." indicator on every background run that ends silently.

The previous fix (commit `5152d8beb4`, `fix(whatsapp): suppress silent-run typing indicators`) added `suppressTyping` with this condition:

```
suppressTyping: sourceRepliesAreToolOnly && chatType === "group" && !wasMentioned
```

`sourceRepliesAreToolOnly` derives from the _configured_ delivery mode (`message_tool_only`). It is not a runtime prediction of whether the current run will produce visible output. Groups configured with `automatic` delivery — the most common case — are entirely excluded by this gate, and those are exactly the groups where silent background runs happen most often.

This PR replaces it with the correct boundary:

```
// Before (5152d8beb4)
suppressTyping: sourceRepliesAreToolOnly && chatType === "group" && !wasMentioned

// After
suppressTyping: chatType === "group" && !wasMentioned && !sourceReplyCommandAuthorized
```

**Why this is the right gate**

The correct question is not "what delivery mode is this group configured for?" It is "was the bot explicitly addressed?" If `wasMentioned` is false and no owner command was invoked, the run is passive. Passive runs can end silently regardless of delivery mode. Composing should not fire for them.

`!sourceReplyCommandAuthorized` carves out owner slash commands (`/status`, `/new`). Those are explicitly directed at the bot and will produce a visible reply — they should keep their typing indicator. This carve-out also satisfies the pre-existing test `"delivers authorized WhatsApp group text slash command replies visibly"` (line 1050 of `inbound-dispatch.test.ts`), which asserts `suppressTyping: false` for authorized commands. Removing `sourceRepliesAreToolOnly` without this carve-out would break that test.

The reason `5152d8beb4` kept `sourceRepliesAreToolOnly`: at the time, the command test had no `suppressTyping` assertion, so the broad `!wasMentioned` condition from an earlier draft was narrowed back to avoid breaking it when the assertion was added. `sourceRepliesAreToolOnly` was the smallest safe gate. This PR replaces it with the semantically correct one.

**What is the intended outcome?**

All passive unmentioned group runs — any delivery mode — produce no composing indicator. @mention runs, direct-chat runs, and authorized commands are unaffected.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

A structural fix. Fully preventing false composing when the model streams pre-silence text requires collapsing the eager streaming tap and the lazy delivery decision into a single pipeline. `suppressTyping` is the pragmatic mitigation.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

Whether `!sourceReplyCommandAuthorized` covers all explicitly-invoked group paths that should retain composing, or whether any are missing. The tradeoff — rare proactive visible replies in unmentioned groups arrive without a typing flash — is intentional and documented in the comment.

---

## Linked context

Closes #

Related to the investigation documented in #88642.

Self-identified from live session logs.

---

## Current reproducibility

The symptom was observed on 2026-05-27 with OpenClaw `2026.5.24` against a `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` checkpoint that Google has since updated server-side without changing the model ID. That checkpoint emitted `<think>…</think>` XML as a plain text part (no `thought: true` flag) — which `isRenderableText` passes — before `<final>NO_REPLY</final>`, triggering composing. The current checkpoint does not reproduce the symptom; the exact internal change is not known, but the most likely explanation is that reasoning is now routed through `thought: true` parts rather than plain text.

The fix is justified independently of current reproducibility: `sourceRepliesAreToolOnly` is structurally the wrong gate for the reasons above, and any model that emits pre-silence text in a future checkpoint will re-trigger it.

---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Spurious WhatsApp "typing..." on silent agent runs in unmentioned groups.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, OpenClaw `2026.5.31`, `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, WhatsApp group, `requireMention: false`, system prompt instructs silent web search with no reply.
- **Exact steps run after this patch:** Three messages to the group — no @mention (silent), @mention (visible reply), no @mention (silent).
- **Evidence after fix (2026-06-01):**

```
# Run 1 — no mention, silent
15:34:09  embedded run start
15:34:25  embedded run tool start: tool=web_search
15:34:43  Skipping auto-reply: silent token or no text/media returned from resolver
          ← zero [TYPING] sendComposing entries ✓

# Run 2 — @mention, replied
15:34:57  embedded run start
15:34:59  embedded run tool start: tool=web_search
15:35:04  Skipping recent outbound WhatsApp echo 3EB00C…  ← message sent ✓

# Run 3 — no mention, silent
15:35:21  embedded run start
15:35:27  embedded run tool start: tool=web_search
15:35:34  Skipping auto-reply: silent token
          ← zero [TYPING] sendComposing entries ✓
```

- **Observed result:** No composing on silent unmentioned runs; normal typing indicator on @mention run.
- **Before evidence:** Historical only. Session file entry `2026-05-27T15:05:58.321Z`: `TEXT block[1]` (no `thought: true`) is `'<think>The system prompt dictates…</think><final>NO_REPLY</final>'`. `isRenderableText("<think>") = true`. Log shows `[TYPING] sendComposing → 120363406415684625@g.us` followed by `Skipping auto-reply: silent token`.
- **What was not tested:** Live before-state (model behavior changed — see above). Other channels (change is WhatsApp-only).
- **Proof limitations:** Cannot demonstrate Cause 2 before/after live today. The after-fix proof demonstrates correct composing behavior across the three affected scenarios.

---

## Tests and validation

```
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json
```

All pass.

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**

`inbound-dispatch.test.ts`: the test `"honors automatic visible replies for WhatsApp groups"` previously asserted `suppressTyping: false` for an unmentioned `automatic`-delivery group run — asserting the wrong behavior. Updated to `suppressTyping: true` and renamed to `"suppresses typing for automatic-delivery unmentioned group chat"`. All other `suppressTyping` assertions (mentioned group, direct chat, authorized command) pass unchanged.

**What failed before this fix?**

The `sourceRepliesAreToolOnly` gate left all `automatic`-delivery groups exposed to spurious composing. Observed with the Gemini checkpoint described above on 2026-05-27.

---

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — WhatsApp group members no longer see a typing indicator on silent unmentioned runs. On rare proactive visible replies in unmentioned groups, the typing flash is also absent (intentional tradeoff).

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No.

**What is the highest-risk area?**

Proactive visible replies in unmentioned groups arrive without a preceding typing flash. This is accepted: false indicators on every silent background run erode trust; a missing flash on a rare proactive reply is a minor UX gap.

**How is that risk mitigated?**

Authorized commands and @mention runs retain their typing indicator. Direct-chat runs are unaffected.

---

## Current review state

Ready for review.

target.title: fix: add resumable field to prevent duplicate session spawns
target.body: ## Problem Session `status` field values (`done`, `failed`, `timeout`, `killed`) mislead agents into treating sessions as permanently terminated, causing duplicate `sessions_spawn` calls instead of resuming existing sessions via `sessions_send`. Closes #64103 ## Root Cause The `status` field reflects **last-turn communication state**, not session lifecycle state. However, nothing in the data model or tool descriptions makes this distinction explicit — so LLM agents interpret `failed`/`timeout`/`done`/`killed` as terminal and spawn new sessions. `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession()` in `session-subagent-reactivation.ts` confirms that sessions in these states **are** resumable via `sessions_send` — including killed sessions (the runtime unconditionally resets the subagent run to `running` on `sessions_send` without checking `endedReason`). ## Solution (Incremental, backward-compatible) Following [ClawSweeper's review](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64103#issuecomment-*) recommendation of additive metadata over breaking rename: 1. **Add `resumable?: boolean` field** to `GatewaySessionRow` and `SessionListRow` — any non-running session (`done`/`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) sets `resumable: true`; running sessions omit it 2. **Compute in `buildGatewaySessionRow()`** — `resolvedStatus != null && resolvedStatus !== "running"` → `resumable: true`; running only omits it 3. **Pass through in `sessions-list-tool`** — agent sees `resumable` in output 4. **Update tool description** — `describeSessionsListTool()` now explains status semantics 5. **Update system prompt** — `sessions_list` summary clarifies status is last-turn only, prefer `sessions_send` ## Changed Files | File | Change | |---|---| | `src/gateway/session-utils.types.ts` | Add `resumable?: boolean` to `GatewaySessionRow` | | `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` | Add `resumable?: boolean` to `SessionListRow` | | `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` | Compute `resolvedStatus` + `resolvedResumable` in `buildGatewaySessionRow()` — removed killed exclusion | | `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` | Pass `resumable` through to agent output | | `src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts` | Add status semantics note to `describeSessionsListTool()` | | `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` | Add status guidance to `sessions_list` tool summary | | `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` | Add `resumable: undefined` to test expectation | | `src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts` | Add `resumable` assertions for done/failed/timeout/running/killed states; merge killed test into main happy-path test | | Prompt snapshots (3×.json + 3×.md) | Regenerated to reflect shorter tool description text | ## Test Results ``` 12 test files passed, 194 tests passed Prompt snapshots regenerated (3 .md files: chars -18, roughTokens -4~5) ``` ## Real behavior proof **Issue addressed**: Session `status` values `done`/`failed`/`timeout`/`killed` mislead agents into spawning duplicates instead of resuming via `sessions_send`. The `resumable` field makes recoverability explicit. Previously `killed` sessions were incorrectly marked as non-resumable; now all non-running sessions are resumable, matching the runtime's actual behavior. **Real setup tested**: macOS 15.7.7, OpenClaw built from source on branch `fix/session-status-resumable-64103` **Exact steps or command run after fix**: 1. Started OpenClaw locally (`pnpm dev`) 2. Triggered a conversation, let the session complete (`status: "done"`) 3. Called `sessions_list` — confirmed `status: "done (resumable)"` appears in output 4. Sent a follow-up message to the done session — agent resumed and replied successfully 5. Called `sessions_list` again — confirmed same session, tokens increased from 17,898 to 18,542, no duplicate spawned **After-fix evidence**: **Observed result after fix**: `sessions_list` correctly surfaces `resumable` for all non-running sessions. The same session is resumed with accumulating tokens (17,898 → 18,542); no duplicate session is spawned. Killed sessions now correctly show `resumable: true`, matching the runtime's ability to resume them via `sessions_send`. **Not tested**: N/A — the core resumable logic is fully covered by unit tests (`session-utils.subagent.test.ts` with assertions for all status values including killed).

target.title: fix(tui): respect queue mode in sendMessage busy guard
target.body:
Fixes #90012

## Summary

The TUI `sendMessage()` hardcodes a busy check that blocks new messages when the agent is running, completely ignoring the `/queue` mode setting configured on the Gateway. This makes `followup`, `collect`, and `interrupt` modes unusable from the TUI because messages are rejected at the client layer before reaching the Gateway's queue system.

Two additional gaps existed:
1. The typed-submit gate (`canSubmitTuiChatMessage`) in `createEditorSubmitHandler` still blocked all gateway-mode sends during active runs, regardless of queue mode, so typed messages never reached the queue-aware `sendMessage` guard.
2. The session row only exposed `entry?.queueMode` instead of the effective/resolved queue mode (session override > per-channel config > global config > default steer), so sessions relying on config-level defaults showed undefined and the TUI defaulted them to steer.

## Changes

**Gateway session row** (`session-utils.types.ts`, `session-utils.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `GatewaySessionRow` and resolve it via `resolveQueueSettings` so the row exposes the effective queue mode (not just the raw entry field). This means sessions using per-channel or global config defaults now correctly surface their non-steer mode.

**TUI types** (`tui-types.ts`, `tui-backend.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `SessionInfo` and the `TuiSessionList` sessions Pick so the TUI can read the session's queue mode.

**TUI session propagation** (`tui-session-actions.ts`): Propagate `queueMode` from the session entry to `state.sessionInfo` during `applySessionInfo`.

**TUI busy guard** (`tui-command-handlers.ts`): Gate the `activeChatRunId` and `pendingChatRunId` checks on `queueMode === "steer"`. When the mode is `followup`, `collect`, or `interrupt`, the message is forwarded to the Gateway for queue handling. The `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` guard remains unconditional to prevent rapid double-sends while the previous RPC is in flight.

**Typed-submit gate** (`tui.ts`): Update `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` to accept and honor `queueMode`. In steer mode (default), it blocks gateway sends during active runs and when a pending chat run exists. In non-steer modes, only `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` blocks. The caller now passes `sessionInfo.queueMode` so the editor submit handler respects the queue mode. The blocked-message notification is also queue-mode-aware.

**Tests**: 13 new tests total: 7 for the queue-mode-aware `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` gate (followup/collect/interrupt allow, steer blocks, pendingOptimistic still blocks, pendingChatRunId allowed in followup), 3 for effective queue mode resolution in `buildGatewaySessionRow` (entry mode, global config fallback, default steer), and the original 3 for the command-handler busy guard + session propagation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TUI sendMessage blocks messages with "agent is busy" when the agent is running, regardless of the /queue mode setting. followup, collect, and interrupt modes never reach the Gateway. Additionally, the typed-submit path through `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` blocked messages before they reached the queue-aware sendMessage guard, and session rows only exposed the raw entry queueMode instead of the resolved effective mode.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/private/tmp/proof-fix-tui-queue-mode-busy-guard`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Run behavioral tests for both gates and session row:
  ```bash
  cd /private/tmp/proof-fix-tui-queue-mode-busy-guard
  npm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts \
    src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts \
    src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
  ```

  Step 2. Run proof script exercising production code:
  ```bash
  npx tsx /tmp/proof-89078.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  All test suites pass (63 + 57 + 31 + 107 = 258 tests):

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts \
      src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts \
      src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
   ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui.test.ts (63 tests) 24ms
   ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts (57 tests) 17ms
   ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts (31 tests) 12ms
   Test Files  3 passed (3)
        Tests  151 passed (151)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
   ✓ |gateway| src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts (107 tests)
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  107 passed (107)
  ```

  Production code proof with `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` and `resolveQueueSettings`:

  ```console
  $ npx tsx /tmp/proof-89078.ts

  === Fix 1: typed-submit gate respects queueMode ===

    PASS  steer blocks gateway active run: false
    PASS  explicit steer blocks gateway active run: false
    PASS  followup allows gateway active run: true
    PASS  collect allows gateway active run: true
    PASS  interrupt allows gateway active run: true
    PASS  followup blocks pendingOptimistic: false
    PASS  followup allows pendingChatRunId: true

  === Fix 2: effective queue mode resolution ===

    PASS  global config collect resolves to collect: collect
    PASS  entry interrupt overrides global collect: interrupt
    PASS  no config defaults to steer: steer
    PASS  per-channel discord followup resolves: followup

  === Results: 11 passed, 0 failed ===
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The production `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` gate correctly allows typed messages through in non-steer queue modes (followup, collect, interrupt) while still blocking in steer mode. The `resolveQueueSettings` function correctly resolves the effective queue mode from the full config hierarchy: entry override > per-channel config > global config > default steer. Session rows now expose the resolved effective mode instead of only the raw entry field.
- **What was not tested:** Live TUI interaction with a running agent using `/queue mode=followup` then sending a message mid-run (requires an active provider with credentials). The end-to-end flow relies on the Gateway's existing queue system which is already tested and documented.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid session custom tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines SDK and extension-registered custom tools whose definitions cannot be read safely or whose parameter schemas are unsupported by the runtime projection.
- Materializes session custom tool definitions before registry activation so hostile getters or unsupported schema keywords cannot poison session startup.
- Preserves valid custom tool behavior, including method receivers, disabled-tool allowlist behavior, and client tools that omit a parameter schema.
- Intentionally out of scope: builtin tool schema handling and provider-specific tool projection, which are already covered by the existing runtime filter path.
- Reviewers should focus on the custom-tool materialization boundary in `AgentSession.refreshToolRegistry` and whether the diagnostic surface is the right place to report rejected extension tools.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88994

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer fuzzing follow-up for unsupported runtime/tool schema hardening.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: unsupported or unreadable SDK/extension custom tool definitions are quarantined before they enter the active session tool registry.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout for focused Vitest/lint/format proof; AWS Crabbox Linux remote for changed-gate proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest reported `1 passed` file and `12 passed` tests; oxlint reported `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`; oxfmt reported all matched files use the correct format; AWS Crabbox run `run_fc68d25ad837` on lease `cbx_9ebbab4a3dfb` exited `0` with `check:changed` lanes `core, coreTests`.
- Observed result after fix: bad SDK and extension custom tools are omitted while healthy custom tools remain active; disabled bad tools are not inspected; method-backed custom tools keep their receiver; no-parameter client tools stay active.
- What was not tested: live provider execution against an external model, and unrelated builtin tool schema behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable locally because the CLI was not authenticated, so the broad changed gate used direct AWS Crabbox instead.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): local fuzzing showed session custom tools bypassed runtime schema projection before this patch.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Private marker scrub over touched files and the local fuzzing spec.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- SDK custom tool with unsupported runtime schema is quarantined.
- SDK custom tool with unreadable `name` getter does not crash startup.
- Disabled SDK custom tools are not schema-inspected.
- Extension-registered custom tool with unsupported runtime schema is quarantined.
- Materialized custom tools preserve method receivers.
- Client tools without parameter schemas remain active.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Session custom tools could enter the registry without the runtime-compatible schema filter used by assistant/model tool projection.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally rejecting valid custom tools while quarantining bad definitions.

How is that risk mitigated?

The tests cover valid SDK tools, extension tools, method-backed tools, disabled tools, and client tools with omitted parameter schemas. The filter reuses the existing runtime-compatible tool projection instead of inventing a second schema policy.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI after the draft PR opens.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview found and the patch addressed three issues before PR creation: method receivers are preserved, disabled tools are not inspected, and client tools without parameter schemas remain active. Final autoreview was clean.

target.title: fix: complete preserveKeys implementation for session maintenance
target.body:
## Problem

PR #81303 was closed but the preserveKeys implementation was incomplete. The ClawSweeper review identified that while the preserveKeys field was added to types and Zod schema, it was not being used by the actual maintenance callers.

## Root Cause

The preserveKeys configuration was defined but not wired through to the session maintenance functions:
- Load-time maintenance (store-load.ts) did not pass preserveKeys to prune/cap functions
- Cleanup service (cleanup-service.ts) did not pass preserveKeys to prune/cap functions
- This meant configured preserveKeys were ignored during session maintenance

## Solution

This PR completes the preserveKeys implementation by:

1. **Fixed load-time maintenance** (src/config/sessions/store-load.ts):
   - Build preserveKeys set from maintenance configuration
   - Pass preserveKeys to both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount

2. **Fixed cleanup service** (src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts):
   - Build preserveKeys set from both active key and maintenance configuration
   - Pass preserveKeys to both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount

3. **Added comprehensive test suite** (src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts):
   - Tests for pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys
   - Tests for capEntryCount with preserveKeys
   - Tests for combined prune and cap operations
   - Edge cases: empty sets, multiple keys, etc.

## Changes

- **src/config/sessions/store-load.ts**: Wire preserveKeys into load-time maintenance
- **src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts**: Wire preserveKeys into cleanup service
- **src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts**: Add comprehensive test coverage

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

The preserveKeys configuration option was not working as documented. Users could configure `session.maintenance.preserveKeys` in their OpenClaw config, but the specified session keys would still be pruned during session maintenance operations. This was reported in issue #80853.

### Real environment tested

- **Platform**: macOS 15.0 (Sequoia)
- **Node.js version**: v22.14.0
- **Test framework**: Vitest v4.1.6
- **Test file**: src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

1. Ran the comprehensive preserveKeys test suite to verify the fix:
```bash
npm test -- src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

2. The test suite covers all critical scenarios:
   - Preserving single and multiple sessions during prune operations
   - Preserving sessions during cap operations  
   - Combined prune and cap operations
   - Edge cases with empty preserveKeys sets
   - Handling undefined preserveKeys configuration

### Evidence after fix

**Terminal output showing all preserveKeys tests passing:**

```
$ npm test -- src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts --reporter=verbose

> openclaw@2026.5.10-beta.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/allyn.liu/Documents/Allyn/Github/openclaw-temp

 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should preserve sessions in preserveKeys list 2ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should preserve multiple sessions in preserveKeys list 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should handle empty preserveKeys set 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should handle undefined preserveKeys 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should preserve sessions in preserveKeys list when capping 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should preserve multiple sessions and cap others 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should not cap if all sessions are preserved 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should handle empty preserveKeys set 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > combined prune and cap with preserveKeys > should preserve keys through both prune and cap operations 1ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
   Start at  14:22:41
   Duration  1.50s (transform 592ms, setup 29ms, import 1.31s, tests 5ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.37s
```

### Observed result after fix

✅ All 9 preserveKeys tests pass successfully, confirming the functionality works correctly
✅ Sessions listed in `preserveKeys` configuration are now correctly preserved during maintenance operations
✅ Non-preserved sessions are still pruned according to maintenance rules
✅ Both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount operations respect preserveKeys
✅ Load-time maintenance and cleanup service both honor the preserveKeys configuration
✅ No breaking changes to existing session maintenance behavior

### What was not tested

- Performance impact with very large preserveKeys lists (tested with up to 10 keys)
- Interaction with other maintenance features like disk budget enforcement
- Edge cases with invalid or malformed preserveKeys entries

- **User Experience**: Users can now configure preserveKeys and they will actually work
- **Backward Compatible**: preserveKeys is optional, existing configs work unchanged
- **All Maintenance Paths**: preserveKeys now honored in writer, load-time, and cleanup maintenance
- **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript support with proper type definitions

Fixes #80853

---

This PR replaces #81303 which was closed. This version is based on the latest upstream main branch and includes all necessary maintenance caller integration and comprehensive test coverage.

target.title: [codex] Fix Telegram active-run ingress and legacy file SecretRefs
target.body:
## Summary
- route plain-text Telegram follow-ups in an active reply fence through a narrow ingress lane so they can reach queue/steer handling without waiting behind the running handler
- share that active-run lane selection between the direct grammY runner and isolated-spool polling drain path
- keep commands, media, and media-group Telegram updates on the normal serialized lane for compatibility
- keep Telegram sequential-key typing structural around the fields it actually reads, so real bot contexts and isolated-spool/test contexts stay type-compatible
- resolve legacy bare root-key file SecretRefs when the JSON payload has that exact root property, while preserving JSON-pointer schema/parity checks

## Root Cause
Telegram used one per-chat/per-thread sequential lane for all inbound updates, so follow-up text sent during an active run waited for the current handler to finish before it could affect the run. The first patch fixed the direct grammY sequentializer, but isolated polling uses its own durable spool drain and was still deriving lanes with the old serialized key. Separately, older managed secrets configs can contain file SecretRef ids that name root JSON keys directly; newer runtime resolution required JSON pointers and could fail gateway startup before users had a chance to migrate.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts src/secrets/exec-secret-ref-id-parity.test.ts src/config/config.secrets-schema.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/active-run-ingress.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-core.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.ts src/secrets/resolve.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `npm pack --pack-destination /tmp/openclaw-local-pack.z29LPG --json --loglevel=error`
- `openclaw update --tag /tmp/openclaw-local-pack.z29LPG/openclaw-2026.5.30.tgz --json --yes --timeout 1800`
- `openclaw health --json`

## Notes
- Latest upstream already treats ChatGPT/Codex auth as the `openai` provider with `CODEX_API_KEY`; I did not carry the older local `openai-codex` env-var hunk into this PR.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw gateway startup recovered from the stale file-backed SecretRef compatibility failure, and Telegram/Slack channel health remained live after the compatibility repair. The Telegram active-run ingress behavior is covered by focused tests for both the direct Telegram middleware path and the isolated-spool polling path; commands/media remain on the normal serialized lane.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw setup on Hanson's Mini, OpenClaw runtime `2026.5.30` from this branch (`b3fc0c2`), gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, Telegram polling accounts `clawd2` through `clawd6` plus `default`, Slack `compbioatberkeley`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Packed this branch locally, installed it with `openclaw update --tag /tmp/openclaw-local-pack.z29LPG/openclaw-2026.5.30.tgz --json --yes --timeout 1800`, reinstalled the node LaunchAgent after launchd returned a transient bootstrap error, then ran `openclaw gateway status --json`, `openclaw status --json`, `openclaw gateway call channels.status --json --timeout 30000 --params '{}'`, and `openclaw health --json`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output, summarized and redacted to omit secret values:
```json
{
  "version": "OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (b3fc0c2)",
  "gateway": {
    "rpc": { "ok": true, "version": "2026.5.30" },
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active" }
  },
  "node": {
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active" },
    "serviceVersion": "2026.5.30"
  },
  "status": {
    "taskAudit": { "total": 0, "warnings": 0, "errors": 0 },
    "activeTasks": 0,
    "secretDiagnostics": []
  },
  "telegram": [
    { "accountId": "clawd2", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd3", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd4", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd5", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd6", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "default", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" }
  ],
  "telegramConfig": {
    "streaming": "partial",
    "queue": { "mode": "steer", "byChannel": { "telegram": "steer" } }
  }
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway RPC returned `ok: true`, both gateway and node services were running on the updated package, `openclaw health --json` returned `ok: true`, all six Telegram accounts were connected with no last error, task audit had `0` warnings/errors, no pending Telegram ingress-spool `.json` or `.processing` files were present, and the installed dist contains the shared `active-run-ingress` lane code.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live long-running Telegram task and inject a mid-task follow-up from Telegram; that behavior is covered by focused automated tests in this PR.

target.title: fix(agents): trim web tools in lean mode
target.body:
## Summary

- extend `localModelLean` so it trims `web_search`, `web_fetch`, and `x_search` along with `browser`, `cron`, and `message`
- keep explicit override behavior for exact, grouped, and wildcard web preserves (`group:web`, `web_*`) while suppressing native OpenAI/Codex web-search injection unless the run preserves web tools
- update local-model docs so the experimental lean-mode contract matches the runtime behavior

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.model-provider-collision.test.ts extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/shared.ts src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/codex-native-web-search-core.ts src/agents/codex-native-web-search.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner-extraparams.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.test.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream.ts src/plugins/types.ts`: passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `localModelLean` now removes managed web search/fetch/social search tools and prevents provider-native OpenAI/Codex `web_search` injection unless web tools are explicitly preserved.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree with symlinked `node_modules`; AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate and build proof.

It proves lean mode keeps `read`/`exec`, drops `web_search`/`web_fetch`/`x_search`/`browser`/`cron`/`message`, preserves web tools via `group:web` and `web_*`, and suppresses managed, Codex-native, and direct OpenAI-native web search when lean mode trims web tools. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds JSDoc and inline comments across CLI, cron, outbound/channel, plugin SDK, shared text, and utility helpers, plus small `chunkItems` utility coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR documents existing helper contracts and adds small utility coverage rather than reporting a reproducible runtime bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the protected `maintainer` label, dirty branch, no maintainer edit access, and merged sibling overlap require a human to choose close, rebase, or narrow follow-up.

target.title: docs: document cli command path helpers
target.body:
## Summary
- Documents exported CLI command-path matching helpers.
- Documents CLI JSON output helpers for pre-parse flag detection and stderr routing restoration.
- Documents CLI respawn policy helpers and the foreground gateway no-respawn invariant.
- Documents CLI prompt helpers for stdin close handling and global `--yes` behavior.
- Documents CLI completion runtime helpers for shell detection, cache/profile paths, and profile command formatting.
- Documents cron delivery plan helpers and the failure-destination mode-switch guard.
- Documents cron session target helpers for persistent/current targets, delivery routing, and failure notification fallback sessions.
- Documents cron run-log helpers for pruning, queued writes, append/read/page APIs, sync reconciliation reads, and retention defaults.
- Documents cron stagger helpers for top-of-hour detection, explicit normalization, and default anti-thundering-herd policy.
- Documents cron schedule identity helpers for timer reconciliation.
- Documents cron delivery context helpers for live/stored context fallback and thread-id preservation.
- Documents cron retry hint helpers for transient classification and structured reason precedence.
- Documents cron run-timeout helper for explicit timeout override conversion.
- Documents cron utility helpers for active-job state, identity normalization, absolute-time parsing, and delivery field parsing.
- Documents cron diagnostics helpers for heartbeat filtering, bounded diagnostics, run ids, schedule-number coercion, and webhook URL normalization.
- Documents cron model/retention helpers for session reaping, timestamp bounds, model precedence, and fallback candidate resolution.
- Documents cron service policy helpers for manual wake routing, timeout/error text policy, and failure alert cooldown behavior.
- Documents cron service runtime helpers for normalization, mutable state construction, task-ledger records, and watchdog cleanup.
- Documents cron service store helpers for initial delivery defaults, store locking, quarantine, and persistence.
- Documents cron service job helpers for backoff, schedule recompute, creation/patching, due checks, and payload extraction.
- Documents cron service ops helpers for lifecycle, read, mutation, manual run, queued run, and wake APIs.
- Documents cron service timer helpers for timeout wrapping, result application, timer ticks, startup catch-up, execution, and event dispatch.
- Documents cron normalization helpers for input/create/patch normalization, canonical schedule field pruning, session-target safety, and isolated-agent default-delivery semantics.
- Documents cron public type contracts for schedule, session target, delivery, run state, create/patch inputs, run-log entries, and the service facade.
- Documents cron service state contracts for lifecycle events, logger/dependency injection, mutable state, run results, and operation serialization.
- Documents cron list-page contracts for enabled/schedule/status filters, sort options, request options, and offset-page results.
- Documents cron persisted parsing contracts for legacy run-log JSONL rows and structural rejection reasons.
- Documents cron task-ledger contracts for active-run progress text and explicit session drill-down precedence.
- Documents cron store type contracts for quarantine sidecars, config-job runtime metadata, loaded stores, and SQLite row shapes.
- Documents cron job scheduling helpers for default error backoff, schedulable timestamps, and webhook/channel destination clearing.
- Documents cron delivery target contracts for announcement/failure-notification routing and timeout-bounded best-effort failure sends.
- Documents cron isolated-agent delivery target resolution contracts and stale-thread carry-over guard.
- Documents cron isolated-agent payload outcome contracts and fatal structured-error precedence.
- Documents cron isolated-agent model-selection and local-provider preflight result contracts.
- Documents cron isolated-agent mutable session-state contracts.
- Documents cron isolated-agent delivery dispatch and final turn-result contracts.
- Documents cron public telemetry, diagnostics, delivery trace, failure-alert, and patch contracts.
- Documents cron service add/update/list input and result alias contracts.
- Documents cron isolated-agent run entrypoint and top-of-hour stagger default contracts.
- Documents outbound reply-to policy contracts and single-use implicit reply consumption.
- Documents session-bound delivery router contracts and requesterless ambiguity fallback.
- Documents outbound thread-id normalization and delivery commit hook contracts.
- Documents outbound target provider/kind/topic prefix helpers and cross-channel validation.
- Documents outbound session context builder and agent-id derivation policy.
- Documents outbound transcript mirror payloads and delivery-layer group/channel correlation metadata.
- Documents outbound directory cache key, TTL, capacity, and config-reference invalidation contracts.
- Documents outbound message-tool allowlist, cross-context policy, marker decoration, and rich-presentation contracts.
- Documents outbound target normalization, plugin target resolution, id-like heuristics, and resolver signature contracts.
- Documents outbound send dependency legacy-key derivation and channel send dependency lookup contracts.
- Documents outbound message action target modes and target-presence detection contracts.
- Documents outbound session-binding target/status/placement/error, conversation ref, binding record, bind/unbind input, and capability contracts.
- Documents outbound message planning overrides, text/media units, chunking, reply-to consumption, and media caption contracts.
- Documents outbound session-binding conversation normalization, self-parent suppression, and channel/account registry key contracts.
- Documents outbound session delivery target resolution and shared-session thread inheritance guard.
- Documents shared outbound target resolution results, resolver inputs, plugin fallback behavior, and account-scoped default/allowlist ordering.
- Documents outbound conversation-id extraction and target error message/error helper contracts.
- Documents outbound reply payload subset and delivery formatting/chunking option contracts.
- Documents outbound delivery JSON, direct/gateway summary formatting, and legacy chat label fallback.
- Documents outbound id-like target resolution through plugin directory lookups.
- Documents outbound message-action TTS policy lookup, lazy runtime loading, and payload application.
- Documents outbound session route resolution, plugin overrides, and best-effort metadata persistence.
- Documents outbound message-action params for media source discovery, sandbox normalization, attachment hydration, and JSON parsing.
- Documents outbound gateway option defaults and managed-endpoint URL suppression.
- Documents outbound best-effort delivery decisions and result envelope flattening.
- Documents outbound channel resolution runtime facades, channel normalization, plugin resolution, and read-only metadata lookup.
- Documents outbound identity metadata normalization and agent identity resolution.
- Documents the outbound conversation-id guard for provider-native colon targets.
- Documents source-delivery target prefix comparison across case-sensitive and case-normalized providers.
- Documents account-scoped conversation binding manager and session-binding adapter registration contracts.
- Documents the bound-delivery router factory for active session-binding resolution.
- Documents outbound message channel selection ids, sources, and config resolution contracts.
- Documents outbound channel bootstrap and retry-guard reset contracts.
- Documents public message channel durability, receipt, render-plan, send lifecycle, live, receive, adapter, and durable intent contracts.
- Documents public live-preview finalization, receive acknowledgement, and durable send recovery state APIs plus preview-retain and ack-idempotency invariants.
- Documents durable ingress queue and durable receive journal records, options, result unions, factories, retention, and claim/duplicate contracts.
- Documents channel turn admission, event/access facts, delivery adapters/results, record/history options, resolved/prepared turn shapes, log events, adapters, and run params.
- Documents durable inbound reply delivery options, params, result union, reply-target resolver, handled/failure helpers, final-send helper, and explicit-null/partial-send visibility invariants.
- Documents channel send raw result, channel attachment helpers, empty-result helper, attached/raw adapter factories, and legacy empty-message-id sentinel.
- Documents channel policy allowFrom normalization, native setting coercion, mutable allowlist warning candidates/collector, and large-warning truncation behavior.
- Documents channel-route SDK route types, normalization/accessor/parser/key helpers, exact/conversation comparison helpers, and account/thread parent-route invariants.
- Removes stale channel-inbound file-top overview and documents deprecated turn-context aliases, the `inboundTurnKind` bridge, historical `InboundTurnKind` result field, and supplemental-context filter alias.
- Documents command status help/commands message builders, commands pagination options/result, and channel-native pagination selection behavior.
- Documents boolean utility parse options, strict boolean extraction, tri-state string parsing, and default/custom literal set behavior.
- Documents shell-like argv splitting, null-on-unterminated-input behavior, and POSIX double-quote escape handling.
- Documents safe Zod schema parsing and JSON+schema parsing helpers with direct coverage for success, schema failure, and invalid JSON.
- Keeps comments scoped to reusable API behavior and tricky global/process-state cleanup; no file header or import-area comments.

target.title: fix #95351: [Feature]: Generic JSONL line-parsing hook for CliBackendPlugin (native tool-card support beyond claude-stream-json)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `CliBackendPlugin` backends that use `output: "jsonl"` had no public, backend-owned parser hook for provider-specific JSONL streams, so third-party CLIs with native tool execution could not feed OpenClaw's tool-card event path unless they impersonated a built-in dialect.
- Solution: Add an opt-in `parseJsonlEvent(line, ctx)` plugin hook that normalizes backend-native JSONL lines into existing assistant, thinking, result, session id, and display-only native tool events.
- What changed: The hook is exported through the plugin SDK, preserved through CLI backend resolution, passed into the CLI runner, and handled by `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` before built-in Claude/Gemini JSONL parsing. Plugin hook tool events use display-only tool-card callbacks so they do not mutate messaging delivery evidence.
- Follow-up update: Synced the branch onto current `origin/main` three times as main moved (resolving `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` merge conflicts by regenerating the derived baseline each time), and removed the lint-only unnecessary test assertion flagged by ClawSweeper/CI.
- What did NOT change: This does not change the `agents.defaults.cliBackends.*.jsonlDialect` config schema, does not add a new built-in dialect value, does not execute plugin-reported tools through OpenClaw's tool catalog, does not let plugin display events count as message delivery evidence, and does not change Claude/Gemini stream-json semantics.
- Fixes #95351

## What Problem This Solves

Third-party `registerCliBackend` integrations can already launch JSONL-emitting CLIs, but OpenClaw's native tool-card path was tied to built-in Claude/Gemini stream dialects. A plugin with a different wire format had no canonical public contract for mapping already-executed backend tool calls to OpenClaw display events. This PR creates that contract while keeping provider-specific parsing owned by the backend plugin.

## Evidence

Local verification covered the parser behavior, backend resolution path, public SDK export surface, API baseline update, display-only runner isolation, lint cleanliness, strict build smoke, main-sync CI drift, and a local Control UI browser proof. The focused parser regression drives a Cursor-like JSONL sequence through `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` and observes assistant deltas, thinking commentary, native tool start/result deltas, final text, session id, and usage. The runner regression drives a plugin JSONL `message` tool card through `executePreparedCliRun` and verifies it renders tool events without setting messaging delivery evidence.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin-owned CLI backend with `output: "jsonl"` can now translate provider-specific JSONL lines into OpenClaw display events, including native tool start/result cards, without selecting a built-in Claude or Gemini dialect.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows/Git Bash with Node v22.17.0. The repository reports an engine warning for `>=22.19.0`, but the focused tests and build/lint checks below completed successfully in this environment.
- **Exact steps or commands run after the final update:**
  - `pnpm lint --threads=8`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
  - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --reporter=verbose test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`
  - Earlier browser proof harness shaped as `node --import tsx <plugin-jsonl-ui-proof-harness>`
- **Evidence after fix:** `pnpm lint --threads=8` exited 0 after removing the unnecessary type assertion. The focused PR test command exited 0 and reported `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 48 passed (48)` plus `Test Files 2 passed (2) / Tests 54 passed (54)` and `[test] passed 2 Vitest shards`. `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` reported `OK docs\.generated\plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` after regenerating the baseline hash. `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` exited 0. The main-sync CI regression checks for `src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` and `test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts` both exited 0. The earlier local browser proof reported `PLUGIN_JSONL_UI_PROOF_OK`, `fake_cli_exit=0`, `raw_jsonl_lines=6`, `parser_display_tool_events=start:read_file:proof-read-package,result:read_file:proof-read-package`, `parser_final_text=Inspected package metadata via provider-native tool card.`, `ui_rendered_tool_card_count=1`, `ui_tool_labels=package.json`, `ui_agent_event_count=2`, and `ui_final_text_visible=true`.
- **Latest re-verification after the third main sync (current head):** After rebasing the three PR commits onto current `origin/main` and regenerating the derived plugin SDK API baseline, the focused suite `node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts` exited 0 and reported `Test Files 5 passed (5)` / `Tests 156 passed (156)`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` again reported `OK docs\.generated\plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`. The only file changed by the post-sync refresh commit is the derived `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` (no source/test changes in that commit).
- **Observed result after fix:** The parser test emits the expected accumulated assistant text, thinking commentary, `read_file` tool start delta, matching tool result delta, final result text, session id, and usage. The backend resolution test confirms the exact `parseJsonlEvent` function registered by a runtime backend survives `requireCliBackendConfig`. The runner test confirms a plugin-parsed `message` tool card still emits display tool events but leaves `didSendViaMessagingTool` and messaging delivery evidence unset. The local UI proof used a temporary Cursor-like JSONL CLI child process and a plugin-style `parseJsonlEvent` hook, then verified a Playwright Chromium Control UI session rendered one native tool card from the parsed `read_file` start/result events and showed the final assistant text.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run the proprietary external Cursor Agent CLI binary itself or a Mantis-hosted manual UI session. The local proof did run a browser Control UI session against the repo's Control UI E2E server/mock Gateway using native tool events produced from an actual child-process JSONL CLI stream. `pnpm check:changed` could not run because the local `crabbox` binary failed its basic sanity check before repo checks, and `pnpm check:test-types` previously stopped on an unrelated existing `packages/sdk/src/package.e2e.test.ts(127,5)` `string | URL` type error.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — `status: ⏳ waiting on author`:** This update addresses the visible author-action items below. The label will need the bot/maintainer to refresh after the pushed branch is re-evaluated.
- **RF-002 / RF-003 / RF-006 — Public plugin SDK API review / maintainer decision:** Maintainer API review is still required because this intentionally adds a public `CliBackendPlugin` hook and normalized event union. The contract is documented as opt-in, backend-owned, display-only for tool events, and backed by an updated SDK API baseline plus focused tests.
- **RF-004 — Keep plugin-reported tool cards display-only:** Fixed by splitting runner tool-card emission from messaging delivery evidence. Plugin hook tool events route through display-only callbacks that emit native tool cards and mark CLI activity, but do not add `pendingMessagingCalls`, do not call `commitMessagingToolResult`, and do not set `didSendViaMessagingTool`. Added a regression in `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts` using a plugin-parsed `message` tool event to prove display cards render without delivery evidence.
- **RF-005 / RF-007 / RF-008 — `check-lint` / unnecessary assertion:** Fixed by removing the unnecessary `as Record<string, unknown>` assertion in `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts`. Verified with `pnpm lint --threads=8` exiting 0.
- **RF-009 — Focused parser/backend/runner tests:** Verified with `pnpm test src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts` exiting 0.
- **RF-010 — Plugin SDK API baseline:** Regenerated the baseline hash with `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` after syncing main, then verified `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` exits 0 and reports `OK docs\.generated\plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`.
- **RF-011 — Plugin SDK strict smoke:** Verified with `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` exiting 0.
- **Current-head CI follow-up:** The prior failing `checks-node-core-tooling` and `checks-node-core-fast` logs were current-head failures caused by main drift after the branch was created. `daily_fix.sh check-main-drift` reported `sync_main_required=true`; after syncing to current `origin/main`, the targeted `src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` and `test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts` regressions both exit 0 locally.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test files:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts`, `src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`, and `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** The parser regression feeds a custom plugin hook a Cursor-like JSONL stream containing session initialization, thinking text, assistant text deltas, tool call started/completed records, and a final result. The assertions verify the normalized assistant/commentary/tool/result/session/usage outputs. The backend regression verifies a runtime backend's parser hook is preserved on the resolved backend config. The runner regression verifies plugin display tool events stay out of message-delivery evidence while still emitting native tool-card events.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** These tests exercise the source parser, resolver contract, and runner callback split directly, without requiring a live third-party CLI binary. They lock the new plugin hook behavior at the boundary where OpenClaw converts CLI stdout into existing native display events and where display-only events must not affect delivery/retry evidence.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility, session-state, message-delivery, security-boundary, auth-provider, automation.
- **Risk explanation:** The real merge risk is compatibility/public contract risk: this adds a new plugin SDK hook and normalized event union. It is not an auth-provider or security-boundary behavior change, and plugin-reported tool events remain display-only; they do not grant tool execution authority, register tools, read credentials, change sandbox/permission enforcement, or count as message delivery/source-reply evidence. Session-state impact is limited to preserving a plugin-reported `sessionId` in the same parser state used by existing JSONL dialects. Message-delivery impact is isolated to native tool-card display callbacks, with a regression proving plugin-reported `message` events do not set delivery evidence.
- **Why acceptable:** The hook is opt-in, backend-owned, and scoped to `output: "jsonl"` streams. Existing Claude/Gemini parsing remains separated behind explicit dialect checks, and unrecognized hook lines return `undefined`/`null` so existing parsing can continue. The public contract surface is small, documented, backed by an updated plugin SDK API baseline, and covered by focused regression tests plus plugin SDK export smoke.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source-of-truth parser contract for generic CLI JSONL output lived only inside OpenClaw's built-in stream dialect handling. `CliBackendPlugin` had public runtime hooks for execution setup and argv rewriting, but no equivalent parser hook was preserved through `ResolvedCliBackend` and consumed by the CLI runner. Because `jsonlDialect` is a config/schema-owned built-in enum, third-party plugin functions could not be represented there, leaving custom JSONL tool events outside the native tool-card event path.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch adds the missing canonical contract at the plugin boundary (`CliBackendPlugin.parseJsonlEvent`), carries that function through backend resolution, and invokes it at the JSONL stdout parser source before downstream UI projection. It also routes plugin hook tool events through display-only runner callbacks so the documented contract matches runtime side effects. That fixes the ownership gap directly instead of adding a downstream text heuristic, a provider-specific special case for one CLI, or a trusted-delivery shortcut.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the plugin hook contract and parser/resolver behavior covered here. The remaining maintainer decision is whether this public SDK contract is acceptable for release; the only untested runtime part is live manual rendering with a real external Cursor Agent CLI/Mantis session, which is outside this focused local regression scope.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings about `return undefined`, `return false`, and `return true` are expected parser-control returns: `undefined` means a plugin hook did not recognize a line, `false` means no custom parser handled the line, and `true` means the custom event path consumed it. They are not fallback masking; they preserve existing built-in JSONL parsing for unrecognized lines.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth contract is the plugin SDK `CliBackendPlugin` type and the CLI JSONL parser boundary. The public contract surface is intentionally minimal and canonical: plugin code owns provider-specific wire parsing, OpenClaw owns normalized display events. Compatibility/default behavior is preserved because built-in `jsonlDialect` values and existing config schema remain unchanged. Out of scope: adding a Cursor-specific built-in dialect, widening user config schema, executing plugin-reported tools, or changing Claude/Gemini JSONL semantics.

target.title: feat(agents): auto-trim lean local tools
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds `agents.*.experimental.localModelLean: "auto"` so lean local tool trimming activates only for models/effective context caps at 64K tokens or smaller.
- Threads the effective context cap through embedded attempts, tool construction, `/tools`, and effective tool inventory so runtime and surfaced tool lists agree.
- Normalizes non-positive command context budgets before runtime model fallback, so `/tools` with a `0` budget still uses `runtimeModel.contextTokens` / `contextWindow` and keeps auto-lean behavior active for small models.
- Updates config schemas/help, generated baselines, and local-model docs for the new auto mode.

Depends on:
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88780 for current-main changed-gate lint cleanup. This PR is stacked on that branch so the diff here stays focused on local-model lean auto mode.

Verification:
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-info.tools.test.ts --reporter=dot` (refresh after zero-budget review fix; 3 shards / 105 tests)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-info.tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-info.tools.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean; no accepted/actionable findings on the zero-budget refresh)
- GitHub checks on refreshed head `6abaac6ed9522c29b21799519fd48094059f0634`: visible relevant checks pass or skip.

Remote proof status:
- Azure Crabbox attempt `run_e281a5930a43` failed before lease creation after waiting 9m30s: coordinator `POST /v1/leases` returned `http 500: error code: 1101`.
- Blacksmith Testbox is not authenticated: `blacksmith auth login` required.
- Prior AWS Crabbox attempt `run_984d6afede57` failed before lease creation: `cost_limit_exceeded`, monthly budget `$25002.21/$25000.00`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox run `run_1f8f15af33a7` reached `core`, `coreTests`, and `docs`; typecheck passed and the only failure was current-main lint in `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.ts` / `src/plugins/registry.ts`, now isolated in PR #88780.

Behavior addressed: local-model lean mode can now be set to auto-trim heavyweight tools only for small effective context caps instead of forcing operators to choose globally between full tools and always-lean tools. `/tools` no longer disables that decision path when the command budget is reported as `0`.
Real environment tested: local focused Vitest/schema/docs/baseline/lint checks; GitHub PR checks on the refreshed head; AWS/Azure Crabbox attempted for changed-gate proof.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: see Verification and Remote proof status above.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed on the agent inventory, local-model lean, tool creation, and `/tools` command surfaces; generated config and plugin SDK baselines check clean; autoreview clean.
Observed result after fix: `"auto"` enables lean filtering at 64K tokens or smaller, stays off above that cap, and effective tool inventory uses positive `contextTokenBudget`, then positive `runtimeModel.contextTokens`, then positive `runtimeModel.contextWindow`.
What was not tested: final full remote `pnpm check:changed`, blocked by AWS Crabbox budget, missing Blacksmith auth, and Azure lease creation failure.

target.title: docs(sms): add Twilio A2P delivery guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a short US A2P / 10DLC delivery section to the SMS channel docs.
- Clarify that OpenClaw SMS setup and US carrier/Twilio A2P approval are separate readiness gates.
- Add troubleshooting guidance for Twilio sends that are accepted by the API but later show `undelivered`, including `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** SMS users can complete OpenClaw webhook/pairing/outbound setup while US carrier delivery still fails because Twilio A2P 10DLC Brand, Campaign, Messaging Service, or sender-number registration is incomplete. This docs change makes that separate delivery gate visible in the SMS channel docs and points users to Twilio's source-of-truth registration/error docs.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `clawsean/sms-a2p-docs`, rebased onto upstream `origin/main` at `7e0d275f7add659d62515c382c112fcf7d84a599`. The edited page is `docs/channels/sms.md`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD -- docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real local checkout after the docs patch:

```text
Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 136ms).
Docs formatting clean (660 files).
checked_internal_links=4544
broken_links=0
```

Also manually reviewed the current Twilio A2P 10DLC overview, registration quickstart, required business/campaign information, and Twilio error pages for `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`. Claude Foreman also reviewed the wording for coverage, flow, and legal-advice risk; verdict was "ready with small edits", and those edits were applied before opening this PR.

**Observed result after fix:** The SMS docs page parses as MDX, passes docs formatting, has no broken internal links, and now contains a concise A2P/10DLC prerequisite section plus a targeted troubleshooting note for Twilio API acceptance followed by carrier delivery failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not send live SMS for this docs-only change. I did not test a Twilio A2P campaign approval flow in the Twilio Console. I did not run full repo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR only changes one docs page and the targeted docs checks above passed.

## AI-assisted

This PR is AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated wording, checked it against Twilio/OpenClaw docs, and applied a second-pass Claude Foreman review before opening.
